title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, Preconditions, and Status · Compiler Case: Write Method Status Location
summary: A task API uses POST for every action, changes state on GET, returns 200 for rejected work, and retries duplicate writes without resource or precondition semantics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts write method status location increment in resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, status, Location, representation, and asynchronous completion behavior from the resource contract.
Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
